meaning: n.腌食用小黄瓜,黄瓜之一种
examples: The glass-and-steel tower will be built next door to the Gherkin landmark—a pickled cuke-like structure built in 2004.
Architect Robin Partington has the Gherkin, the Razor (right), the Armadillo in Glasgow, and the Cucumber (above) on his CV.
The Gherkin is cunningly crafted out of two types of melon and embedded with green beans to highlight its renowned spiraling glass frames.
Mr. GHERKIN: Well, I prefer Allen, but I think I'm going to vote for Webb.
Mr. SCOTT GHERKIN: In the Lord's eyes, I think marriage is a man and a woman.
